Go hit these accounts and so forth. Right? And it's always hard to track. I mean, there's ways to do it, but it's always hard to really track the impact of the STR outbound efforts. So I know we're pulling in, so for example, we have specific sequences in, in outreach for those campaigns, right? I can see the sequences here. I'll show you what I'm talking about.

political_tussles.tension_label: Attribution model ownership and bonus alignment

political_tussles.quote: My concern would be like, Hey, if we roll out the AI model and we say like, Hey, we're going to score you based on, you know, attribution across events. And we're expecting you to hit this level of attribution. And then next week the model updates and it changes exactly like it changes how it's looking at. Technically, it's doing that because that is more accurate, right? But on the downside, like nobody, like the goalposts is constantly moving and they don't know how to hit their number.

contrarians.claim: Moved AWAY from lead scoring model because lack of confidence in how scores added up
contrarians.why_unusual: Most B2B SaaS companies are moving TOWARD more sophisticated scoring, not abandoning it entirely for crude warm/hot/lava
contrarians.quote: we, we moved away from a scoring model and because there wasn't confidence in how the scores were, you know, adding up.
contrarians.speaker: Jon Moody

contrarians.claim: Reset cookies after 24 hours, deliberately destroying attribution data
contrarians.why_unusual: Standard practice is 30-90 day cookies; 24 hours is extremely aggressive and self-sabotaging for attribution
contrarians.quote: we have our own personal, we, we reset our cookies after 24 hours. So I think that's the first problem that I, I addressed and I'm, I'm, I'm kind of working with our MOPS team to fix as soon as we can to extend that to 60 days.
contrarians.speaker: Jon Moody

contrarians.claim: Running entire business on spreadsheets with no sales forecasting system
contrarians.why_unusual: At their scale and growth rate, complete absence of Clari or similar forecasting tool is unusual; most Series B+ companies have adopted dedicated forecasting platforms
contrarians.quote: We do everything in spreadsheets. Run the whole business outta a spreadsheet.
contrarians.speaker: Ryan Patel-Francis

contrarians.claim: Using campaign member first responded date as primary MQL definition, not lead status history
contrarians.why_unusual: Most attribution vendors recommend lead status history for funnel tracking; Abnormal's campaign-member-based approach creates multiple MQL dates per lead and breaks standard cohort analysis
contrarians.quote: for MEL we are doing, are we using first respond to date for those dashboards that you built in there? Like so that would be campaign, campaign member first respond to date.
contrarians.speaker: Sammy Kiley

contrarians.claim: AI-driven data harmonization and connection suggestions rather than manual field mapping
contrarians.why_unusual: Most attribution platforms require extensive manual schema mapping; RevSure's AI-suggested connections with human verification is differentiated
contrarians.quote: with the AI engine, you can expose the data. This rev share engine will then look at all these different data sources and say, Hey, you have this same formatted thing over here and this same thing, or, or are they the same field? Do you wanna connect them? And so you end up just going through and saying yes, yes, yes, yes, yes. As opposed to manually having to make all these connections, which makes the ramp up time a lot quicker.
contrarians.speaker: Jon Moody

contrarians.claim: Most leads should go through as MQLs regardless of score - only exclude 'flat out junk'
contrarians.why_unusual: Contrary to conventional lead scoring best practice of threshold-based MQL qualification; they deliberately abandoned stricter scoring to appease sales
contrarians.quote: So basically everyone who takes an action who's not just flat out junk or an unsupported country, like things like that, they are going through as an MQL right now. So like the, like not sending through those on the cusp isn't as big because mostly everyone's going through anyways
contrarians.speaker: Sammy Kiley

contrarians.claim: Organic website should have zero budget/ROI attribution since it's a catchall, not a true channel
contrarians.why_unusual: Goes against typical marketing analytics practice of assigning some cost to organic; argues for treating website as infrastructure not media
contrarians.quote: there really has never been organic investment, right? The, the way that we would classify any investment would be to a channel that was a paid channel. The organic is really, it's kind of a catchall
contrarians.speaker: Jon Moody

contrarians.claim: AI propensity scores showing 99% of leads below 5% propensity is not trustworthy - prefer custom heuristic scoring
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors push AI/ML as superior; client explicitly rejects black-box AI for transparent business-rules-based scoring
contrarians.quote: I was looking at that scores and it seemed Harry, that the, let's say out of 20,000 s only about 50 leads were being shown like higher propensity. But the remaining 99%, it was showing less than 5%. So I couldn't trust those numbers. And I, I didn't feel comfortable to write that. We think, you know, we should write those back because it's going to really make everybody feel, should we trust Rev or not?
contrarians.speaker: Bhushan Lengade

contrarians.claim: Marketers shouldn't be expected to use RevSure directly; prefer curated Sigma dashboards instead
contrarians.why_unusual: RevSure's positioning is as marketer-friendly; client explicitly states their marketers are 'pretty face' designers who won't use it
contrarians.quote: our customers are marketers. They're the pretty face, they're the designs and stickers and all that other kind of stuff. They're not gonna be in rsu.
contrarians.speaker: Lizzie Villarreal

contrarians.claim: Excel/Google Sheets integration is more valuable than polished in-app visualization for analysts
contrarians.why_unusual: Most RevTech vendors push their UI/dashboards as superior; client explicitly wants raw data export for spreadsheet analysis
contrarians.quote: I think people who really play with data, they prefer Excel or Google Sheet. Again, I'm not saying you guys need to do this now, but for me it becomes more valuable where when I'm able to export that data into, so see, I'm showing you this, this thing because I basically downloaded the data and then, you know, rearranged it and you know, then brought it, and now I'm able to compare it, right?
contrarians.speaker: Bhushan Lengade

contrarians.claim: Prefer multiple component scores over single monolithic lead score
contrarians.why_unusual: While not extremely rare, Harry's explicit recommendation for decomposed scoring with a master score is a nuanced architectural approach
contrarians.quote: Maybe firmo graphics, user engagement, meaning, you know, page views and form fills and that sort of thing. Maybe another for AI signals. And so you have, you know, those individual component scores and then for the final score you have another score that adds those up. If that makes sense.
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: Last touch attribution may be preferable to AI model for executive reporting because it's defensible and simple
contrarians.why_unusual: Most RevTech vendors push multi-touch or AI attribution as superior; client explicitly wants last touch for board reporting because it's 'solid' not 'flimsy'
contrarians.quote: But I'm accomplishing that hey maybe in Q3 or so we can start to just use, use whichever model they want to use. Whether it's last touch, whoever it's, 'cause you know like the problem is reporting to the higher ups, you can need something that is very solid, right? You can say this is like flimsy, they related they to be so they can stick with last touch whoever is that they wanna do or something that you know for sure is like who source, whoever it is. But like we need to.

contrarians.claim: Awareness campaigns should be measured by account engagement progression rather than direct pipeline attribution
contrarians.why_unusual: Contrary to typical demand gen focus on direct response metrics, client wants to measure awareness campaign success by whether accounts move across intent stages regardless of direct touchpoint causation
contrarians.quote: No, I, I'm not sure about that because that's like, that's a six sense. I mean yes, we, we, we, we could do that, that and see like, okay, accounts that were in awareness stage and take sense or in target stage because early ones, how have they moved? We can look at this or okay, just look at the pod and see, okay, this pod has three ton accounts or four ton accounts. How have these accounts, how many of these accounts have we engaged with? Right? That's kind of is, that's kind of the question you want to know, right? Okay. How, how many get, of course the engagement here will tell us how serious it is, right? If they're engaging at the visitor level, that's good. That's kind of early in science. If it's a lead, that means we got a lead from them. You know, MELM problem means that we got something there. So it doesn't need to, doesn't need to be, I, I want something to compliment or to kind of use it in parallel with what I'm seeing is six sense, it it to kind of confirm if I get asked are we getting account engagements with these accounts in these pod that I can answer the question I can do in six sense, but more take sense not going to be as accurate for me and APAC especially.
contrarians.speaker: Abdallah AlHakim

contrarians.claim: AI attribution model should be deliberately frozen and only refreshed quarterly, not continuously updated, to create stable goals for marketers
contrarians.why_unusual: Most AI/ML vendors pitch real-time continuous learning as a feature; CJ explicitly wants to throttle this to prevent 'moving goalposts' for bonus compensation
contrarians.quote: So what I really want to do is say, how do we look at a multi-touch attribution model that just uses AI to keep things balanced programmatic way rather than, but the only catch there and you guys, we can dive into this more, but maybe in our next call with Greg, that the only catch that I see there is I want to be able to identify at what point we're going to rework that model or have the AI rebalanced. Because if, for example, we say, hey, for Q3, this is what the model is telling us, this is how we're going to measure things and balance things, I'd be interested in your viewpoint on, do we just allow the system to automatically continue to rebalance and we just update people when we see significant shifts? Or people are literally building their plans for doing their marketing to get their attribution based on what we're telling them and how attribution works. If we change that all of a sudden, and it's out of their control, holding them responsible to that number is also hard.

contrarians.claim: Page views should count as synthetic campaigns in attribution even without form fills, to capture awareness impact

contrarians.quote: what we would essentially show is like, hey, marketing is doing a ton of work to generate. all of this awareness let's talk about how that awareness impacts the overall pipeline so we would take page views as obviously a much smaller percentage but still wanted it to show up on the impact right because if we're generating awareness that's the starting point that is a touch then we would have like form fills that's another touch and then we would have right like start but we wanted to include that at least as an element of the attribution
contrarians.speaker: CJ Cunniff

contrarians.claim: AI attribution should be explained as 'more heuristic' rather than 'AI' because the term AI is overused

contrarians.claim: First touch and last touch shouldn't even be called multi-touch attribution

contrarians.claim: It's acceptable to tell customer success stories about agent use cases even if not fully deployed, as directional examples

contrarians.claim: LinkedIn demo ads are less impactful for pipeline than awareness ads
